personas.name: Daniel — Migrant-Worker Dormitory Operator
personas.icp: Operations director at a purpose-built or factory-converted dormitory (1,000–8,000 beds), accountable to MOM dormitory licensing for 24/7 coverage.
pains: ~S$19,400/mo for a 2-officer 24/7 post, rising every year on PWM
MOM licensing makes coverage non-discretionary
Guard attrition leaves posts unfilled
Six-figure command-room CapEx unaffordable per dorm
Night-shift incidents found late

nba.summary: Conventional 2-officer 24/7 dormitory guard post under PWM

personas.name: Serene — Commercial Facilities Owner
personas.icp: Facilities/building manager for a single commercial or industrial property running one manned post plus CCTV.
pains: ~S$9,700/mo for one 24/7 post, lifting on PWM
Lobby guard is a cost tenants notice but rarely value
CCTV recorded but unmonitored
After-hours cover is the weak link
No appetite for a command-room CapEx

nba.summary: One manned 24/7 guard post under PWM

personas.name: Marcus — Boutique Licensed Security Agency Owner
personas.icp: Owner of a Grade-A/B licensed agency (30–150 officers) under PWM margin compression, needing a command-centre story for outcome-based tenders.
pains: OBC tenders demand a command centre capital-rich incumbents have
PWM compresses manpower-contract margins
Clients ask for a 'smart/manless' option he cannot deliver
An in-house command room is a S$500K+ bet
Losing tech-enabled tenders erodes the deployed-officer base

nba.summary: Build or wholesale an equivalent command centre — ~S$650K gross / ~S$275K net, ≈ S$7,600/mo amortised

personas.name: Priya — Internal Elitez Business-Unit Head
personas.icp: Head of an Elitez Group operating unit carrying guarding cost on its own P&L, asked to consume the shared command centre on a cost-allocated charge.
pains: Guarding is the largest controllable cost line and grows with PWM
External contracts give no group-level visibility
The S$650K build needs internal demand to justify the net S$275K
Each BU separately procuring monitoring duplicates spend
No internal benchmark for cost-per-site

nba.summary: Reduced agency guarding + self-run CCTV + ops-admin incident logging

personas.name: Gerald — Large IFM Contract Holder
personas.icp: Bid/operations director at an IFM contractor holding multi-site portfolios, pricing a security scope inside an IFM bid under outcome-based requirements.
pains: Security is the most labour-exposed line in an IFM bid
Multi-site portfolios need one command-and-reporting layer
Procurers score technology and outcomes — pure-manpower bids lose
In-house command capability is non-core and capital-heavy
Foreign-worker quotas cap guarding headcount

nba.summary: Conventional manned guarding across a representative 6-site IFM portfolio

recommended_tiers.name: Sentinel — Commercial Single-Site
recommended_tiers.anchor: ≈ a third of one PWM-loaded 24/7 guard post.

recommended_tiers.name: Garrison — Dormitory Manless Security
recommended_tiers.anchor: ≈ 28% of ~S$19,400/mo conventional guarding — a 60%+ saving, locked.

recommended_tiers.name: Command Partner — CCaaS White-Label
recommended_tiers.anchor: Tender-winning capability vs a ~S$650K self-build; priced as a channel (10 sites included).

recommended_tiers.name: Group Cost-Allocation — Internal BU
recommended_tiers.anchor: Below the BU's own reduced-guarding-plus-CCTV status quo (~S$5,470/site).

pricing_principle: Priced as a guard-post replacement, not as software — every tier anchored to the buyer's PWM-loaded next-best-alternative and set well below it.

grants.name: EDG
grants.note: Co-funds the command-centre adoption as a capability-transformation project.

grants.name: PSG
grants.note: Applies once the package is an IMDA pre-approved solution; subscription buyers.

grants.name: WDG (JR+)
grants.note: Funds the job-redesign of the static-guard role into command-centre operator.

grants.name: SNEF CCP
grants.note: Co-funds reskilling displaced officers into command-centre roles.

open_items: PSG net prices assume an IMDA pre-approved listing (2–4 month process)
PSG/EDG consolidate into the unified EDGE framework H2 2026 — re-verify mechanics
